help.title: Help
help.intro: Slurry Stats saves everything on this device first. Here's the usual flow.

recipe.title: Record a new recipe
steps: Tap New (＋) in the bottom bar and pick Espresso, Brew or Cold brew.
Optionally choose a bean and water profile — add them first if you haven't (see below).
Pick your grinder and enter the grind setting in clicks. Use the Grind converter if you're dialing on a different grinder.
Enter dose in and yield / water out — the ratio is calculated automatically. For espresso the yield is what you weigh in the cup at the end; for brew it's the total water you pour in.

For Brew, build the pour schedule step by step (bloom, pours, agitation) with water and time. To add many pours fast, use Repeat pours (count × grams every N seconds) or the duplicate icon on any step, then tap Apply totals.
Optionally rate it and add flavors/tags now, or log it after brewing.
Tap Save. Your newest recipe appears on Home by default.

brewing.title: Guided brewing
steps: Open a brew recipe and tap Brew for the guided timer. You can set an optional 3s/5s start countdown.
The timer, controls and Log button stay pinned on top while the step list scrolls and follows the current step.
The screen stays awake; it beeps as each step begins, counts down the last seconds before a step ends, and signals when to finish a pour — toggle these in Settings → Brewing.
Tap Mark each time you finish a pour to record real checkpoints.
The brew keeps running even if you leave the screen — resume it any time from the bar above the menu. Tap × to discard it.

coldbrew.title: Cold brew
steps: Pick Cold brew when creating a recipe, then choose a style: Immersion (grounds steeping in a jar/Toddy), Slow drip (a Kyoto/Dutch tower), or Flash (hot-brewed straight onto ice).
Immersion & slow drip set a steep time in hours instead of a pour timer, and use cold water — grind coarse. Flash keeps the hot pour schedule and water temp, just with ice in the carafe.
Optionally add a hot bloom (a small hot pour to kick off degassing before the cold fill), the ice weight, and — for a concentrate — tick Concentrate and set the serving dilution (e.g. 1:3).

On the recipe, tap Start steeping. A persistent pill shows it's brewing and when it'll be ready — it keeps counting even if you close the app (handy on iOS, where you'll want the app on your Home Screen).
When it's done, tap the pill or Log this brew; the actual steep time is filled in for you and saved like any other cup.

logging.title: Logging & history
steps: After brewing, tap Log this brew to rate the cup, score flavors, add tags and — with a refractometer — a TDS reading.
The date & time is filled in for you, but you can edit it to backdate a brew.
If you used the guided timer, your actual brew time is saved and compared against the recipe's planned time.
Every recipe keeps its own brew log, so you can see how the same recipe drifts over time.
Use History to browse all cups, Compare to line up two brews side by side, and Stats for trends.

beans.title: Add beans
steps: Open Beans in the bottom bar, then tap Add.
Enter name, roaster, origin, process and roast date.
Saved beans show up in the bean picker when you create a recipe.

water.title: Add water profiles
steps: Beans → Water profiles tab → Add.
Name it (e.g. Third Wave Water, or Tap) and add supplier and TDS / GH / KH if you know them.
Select it on a recipe to track how water changes the cup.

grinder.title: Grinders & conversion
steps: Beans → Grinders tab. Popular grinders are preloaded; add your own with its microns-per-click.
Use the converter to translate a setting from one grinder to another — it's a starting point, not exact.
Grinders marked “estimate” have approximate values; adjust a few clicks and taste.

sync.title: Backup & sync
steps: Settings → Data: export or import a JSON backup any time.
Optionally connect Supabase and sign in to back up to the cloud and restore on another device.

profile.title: Profile
profile.subtitle: Your identity and brewing defaults
profile.defaultsIntro: Used to prefill a new recipe. Leave blank for none.

install.title: Install Slurry Stats
install.body: Add it to your home screen for one-tap access — and to keep your brews safe from being cleared by the browser.
install.iosHint: Tap Share, then “Add to Home Screen”.

backup.nudgeNever: You haven't exported a backup yet. Keep a copy somewhere safe.
backup.nudgeStale: Your last backup was {{date}}. Export a fresh copy to stay safe.

insights.title: Suggestions
insights.disclaimer: Estimates from your inputs — a guide, not a verdict.
insights.exLow: Estimated extraction {{ext}}% is below the {{lo}}–{{hi}}% sweet spot — grind finer, brew hotter, or extend the time to pull more.
insights.exHigh: Estimated extraction {{ext}}% is above {{lo}}–{{hi}}% — risk of bitterness; grind coarser or shorten the brew.
insights.exGood: Estimated extraction {{ext}}% sits in the {{lo}}–{{hi}}% sweet spot.
insights.tdsLow: Estimated strength {{tds}}% is lighter than the {{lo}}–{{hi}}% target — tighten the ratio (less water) for more body.
insights.tdsHigh: Estimated strength {{tds}}% is stronger than {{lo}}–{{hi}}% — loosen the ratio (more water) to taste.
insights.tdsGood: Estimated strength {{tds}}% is right in the {{lo}}–{{hi}}% target.
insights.ratioTight: A 1:{{ratio}} ratio is tight here (typical {{lo}}–{{hi}}) — expect a heavy, intense cup.
insights.ratioLoose: A 1:{{ratio}} ratio is loose here (typical {{lo}}–{{hi}}) — expect a lighter cup.
insights.tempLow: {{temp}} is on the cool side (typical {{range}}) — may underextract; nudge it up.
insights.steepShort: A {{h}}h steep is short (typical {{lo}}–{{hi}}h) — the cup may taste thin.
insights.steepLong: A {{h}}h steep is long (typical {{lo}}–{{hi}}h) — it can turn woody or over-extracted.
insights.dilutionTip: To land near {{target}}% in the glass, dilute this concentrate roughly 1:{{dil}}.
insights.beanFading: This bean is {{age}} days off roast and starting to fade — grind a touch finer or brew a little hotter to keep extraction up.
insights.beanStale: This bean is {{age}} days off roast — past its best. Expect a flat cup; grind finer to compensate, or reach for a fresher bag.

dialIn.title: Dial-in
dialIn.disclaimer: Suggestions from your last shot — taste is the final judge.
dialIn.empty: Pull a shot and log it (time, yield, and taste) to get your next grind adjustment.
dialIn.fast: Last shot gushed in {{time}}s, faster than your {{lo}}–{{hi}}s target — the puck is letting water through too easily.
dialIn.slow: Last shot dragged at {{time}}s, slower than your {{lo}}–{{hi}}s target — flow is choked.
dialIn.sour: Shot time looks good at {{time}}s but it tasted sour — it's under-extracted. Raise the temperature and tighten the grind slightly.
dialIn.bitter: Shot time looks good at {{time}}s but it tasted bitter — it's over-extracted. Drop the temperature and open the grind slightly.
dialIn.dialed: Dialed in — {{time}}s and balanced. Lock this grind setting into the recipe.

coldbrew.flashHint: Hot brew dropped onto ice — keeps the pour schedule below.

gym.title: Pour Gym
gym.intro: Train your pour speed and technique. Follow the moving dot and the sound cues — a tick each second while pouring, a chime at each pulse.
gym.noPours: This recipe has no pour steps to practice.

home.empty: No recipes yet. Brew your first one!
home.firstRunHelp: New here? See how Slurry Stats works

recipe.title: Recipe name
recipe.repeatHint: Generates equal pours, evenly spaced after the last step.

adopt.title: Adopt into original
adopt.hint: Copy the checked values from this fork back into "{{title}}". The fork is left unchanged.
adopt.noDiff: This fork matches its original — nothing to adopt.

log.title: Brew log
log.empty: No brews logged yet. Log one to start tracking how this recipe changes over time.

play.title: Brew
play.noSteps: This recipe has no pour schedule. Add steps to use guided brewing.

session.measuredHint: Got a refractometer? Log a TDS reading to plot your real extraction.

chart.title: Brew control chart
chart.disclaimer: Estimate from your parameters — directional, not a measurement.

stats.title: Stats
stats.empty: Log some brews to see your stats.
stats.avgActual_one: Average actual brew time {{time}} across {{count}} timed brew
stats.avgActual_other: Average actual brew time {{time}} across {{count}} timed brews
stats.onPlan_one: Right on plan across {{count}} brew with a target time
stats.onPlan_other: Right on plan across {{count}} brews with a target time
stats.runsLong_one: Runs {{delta}} long vs plan across {{count}} brew
stats.runsLong_other: Runs {{delta}} long vs plan across {{count}} brews
stats.runsShort_one: Runs {{delta}} short vs plan across {{count}} brew
stats.runsShort_other: Runs {{delta}} short vs plan across {{count}} brews

share.recipeHint: Scan the QR or share the link. It contains the full recipe — no account needed.

import.title: Import recipe
import.invalid: This link doesn't contain a valid recipe.
import.bundledHint: New items are added to your library; existing ones are matched by name.

beans.title: Beans
beans.tooFresh_one: {{name}} may be too fresh — about {{count}} more day of resting.
beans.tooFresh_other: {{name}} may be too fresh — about {{count}} more days of resting.

grinder.title: Grinders
grinder.disclaimer: Starting point only — not exact. Burrs, wear and conical-vs-flat geometry differ. Expect to adjust 1–3 clicks and taste.
grinder.roughWarning: Different burr type (conical ↔ flat): the cup may differ even at the same micron size.
grinder.estimatedWarning: This grinder's microns-per-click is a rough estimate (stepless or sparsely documented). Treat the result loosely.

settings.title: Settings
settings.pourRatesHint: Pour speeds in grams of water per second. Used to show when to finish each pour during a guided brew.
settings.cuesHint: Beeps and haptic buzzes during guided brews and drills.
settings.cueVolumeHint: Crank the beeps up louder or quieter.
settings.stepEndCueHint: Beep down the final seconds before each step ends.
settings.pourMarkCueHint: Beep when the current pour should be finished.
settings.storagePersistedHint: Your brews are protected from being cleared automatically by the browser.
settings.storageBestEffortHint: The browser may clear your data if the device runs low on space. Install the app to your home screen and back up regularly.
settings.backupNever: You haven't exported a backup yet. Export one to keep a copy off this device.
settings.cloudIntro: Slurry Stats works fully offline. Power users can connect Supabase to back up and sync across devices.
settings.driveNote: Back up to your own Google Drive. We only access a private app folder — never your other files — and store nothing on our servers.
settings.driveNoBackup: No backup found in your Drive yet
settings.driveRemoveConfirm: Delete the backup file from your Google Drive? Your data on this device is not affected.
settings.syncOffline: Offline — will sync when back online
settings.signUpCheckEmail: Account created. Check your email if confirmation is required, then sign in.
settings.localFirstNote: Your data is saved on this device first; the cloud is a backup you can restore on another device.
